mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (3-month change) signals broad economic strength and robust corporate earnings growth, leading to positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC labor market recovery has been a key driver of the bull market.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) is negative over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, surprising contraction in retail sales indicates an abrupt slowdown in consumer spending, which negatively impacts revenue expectations for consumer-facing companies and drives negative market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes the largest part of US GDP.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation accelerates while the policy rate remains low, the market perceives the central bank as 'behind the curve'. This creates expectations of future aggressive tightening, increasing discount rates and causing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in CPIAUCSL > 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS < 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Market anxiety about the timing and pace of Fed rate hikes is high in 2016.
failure_condition: The signal fails to generate a statistically significant negative return premium (p > 0.05) during triggered periods compared to non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a very low unemployment rate signals a tight labor market, which leads to wage inflation and prompts pre-emptive monetary tightening by the central bank, negatively impacting equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:The unemployment rate of 4.7% in May 2016 is near estimates of the natural rate of unemployment (NAIRU).
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0%, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is not statistically lower than when UNRATE is above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive year-over-year growth in industrial production reflects a healthy manufacturing and supply side of the economy, supporting corporate profits and leading to positive equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key component of business cycle dating.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 12-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A strong acceleration in housing starts is a leading indicator of broad economic activity and consumer confidence, as it pulls forward demand for materials, labor, and durable goods, boosting forward equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average of HOUST is > 10% above the 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:The housing market was central to the 2008 crisis and its recovery is a key indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: The hit rate (percentage of positive forward returns) following a trigger event is less than the baseline market hit rate of 65.5%.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the effective federal funds rate signals a tightening of financial conditions, which immediately increases the risk-free rate used for discounting and negatively impacts equity prices over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed hiked rates in Dec 2015 for the first time post-GFC, and markets are sensitive to signals of further hikes.
failure_condition: The beta of 5-day forward S&P 500 returns to the 1-month change in FEDFUNDS is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of rising unemployment and falling retail sales is a strong, unambiguous signal of recession. This joint signal indicates both weakening labor markets and consumer demand, leading to sharply negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.3 AND 3-month change in RSAFS < 0
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is non-negative.
